subject: Fwd: contrast: AREtomo vs WARP
body:
Hi all,

Forwarding my own message to the list cause I just noticed I only replied
directly by mistake.

Best,
Lorenzo

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Lorenzo Gaifas <lorenzo.gaifas@gmail.com>
Date: Thu, Nov 23, 2023 at 3:08 PM
Subject: Re: contrast: AREtomo vs WARP
To: mrJ <bluufive@gmail.com>


Hi Mrj,

In my experience, this is typical (no filtering in warp), though it might
also be caused by the alignments being off (many steps along the way could
go wrong, tilt series ordering, angles, and so on).

Do the features in the deconvolved version in warp look more visible at
least?

For a better comparison, you can also just apply a simple gaussian blur to
the warp reconstruction to get rid of the high res noise.

Best,
Lorenzo

On Thu, Nov 23, 2023 at 1:36 PM mrJ <bluufive@gmail.com> wrote:

> dear community,
>
> I have included AREtomo as part of my WARP pipeline in order to get .xf
> file for alignment.  The AREtomo tomograms look quite nice, however when I
> use the .xf file to generate the WARP tomogram I notice the contrast is
> substantially worse.  See image below.  The tomogram looks otherwise
> reasonable, it just has really bad contrast.  For AREtomo I was also using
> WBP (which I think WARP does).
>
> Is this to be expected? is it because WARP has corrected CTF and therefore
> less contrast?
>
> thanks!
>
>
>
> [image: Screenshot 2023-11-23 at 1.35.02 PM.png]
>
